vulnerability.reference: Research benchmarks relying on likelihood metrics overstate model quality for misspecified tokenizers.
vulnerability.generated: Evaluation frameworks treat token-level likelihoods as faithful string probabilities without checking for noncanonical encodings.

impact.reference: Flawed models are published and deployed based on misleading evaluation metrics.
impact.generated: Held-out likelihood metrics become unreliable, misleading researchers about true model performance and hindering progress in language modeling.

dose_response.reference: Tokenization mismatch distorts token probabilities, which in token-level language modeling exposes researchers to diverged likelihood estimates, leading to flawed model publication and wasted research resources.
dose_response.generated: Tokenization mismatch inducing noncanonical encodings, through researchers evaluating with held-out likelihood, triggers evaluation frameworks to treat token-level likelihoods as faithful string probabilities without checking for noncanonical encodings, leading to unreliable metrics that mislead researchers and hinder progress.
